Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To

MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT WILD PLANET FOODS, INC. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To

Off calendar. Dismissal of entire action filed on June 26, 2025.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-30-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To
Off Calendar May-22-2025 Continued To Jul-22-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANT WILD PLANET FOODS, INC.'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Wild Planet Foods, Inc. moves to compel Gabriel Espinoza's further responses to its special interrogatories and requests for production, and seeks sanctions. The initial responses were deficient, but before filing an opposition, Espinoza served amended responses that provided significantly more information than his initial responses. Wild Planet contends the amended responses remain deficient. Wild Planet's motion was likewise deficient in that it did not provide 16 days' notice, although the parties agreed to the hearing date.

The court has discretion to decide a motion to compel, and to award sanctions, notwithstanding that supplemental responses were served while the motion was pending. (See Cal. Rules of Court 3.1030(a).) The court also has discretion to excuse an untimely notice of motion, except in specific contexts like summary judgment. But on the facts presented here, where Espinoza has served substantive responses, the court concludes that it would be better in the first instance to permit the parties to meet and confer and try to narrow any remaining disputes. The court continues the matter for hearing to July 22, 2025 concerning any further responses ordered and any sanctions. The parties are ordered to meet and confer telephonically or in person. Wild Planet may file a supplemental brief of no more than five pages explaining what relief it continues to seek and why no later than two weeks in advance of the continued hearing date. Espinoza may respond in a supplemental brief of no more than five pages no later than one week in advance of the continued hearing date. Wild Planet may take its motion off calendar if the parties resolve their discovery dispute by providing three days' notice to the court.

An order is submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)
